papers.title: Correction to: Supersingular K3 surfaces are unirational
BOUNDS FOR THE STALKS OF PERVERSE SHEAVES IN CHARACTERISTIC  $p$  AND A CONJECTURE OF SHENDE AND TSIMERMAN
A REFINED BRILL-NOETHER THEORY OVER HURWITZ SPACES
Correction to: Representation growth and rational singularities of the moduli space of local systems
HITCHIN FIBRATIONS, ABELIAN SURFACES, AND THE  $\mathbf{P} = \mathbf{W}$  CONJECTURE
Nearby motivic sheaves of weighted equivariant functions
ERRATUM TO “TAME TOPOLOGY OF ARITHMETIC QUOTIENTS AND ALGEBRAICITY OF Hodge LOCI”
Retraction: “Quasi-projectivity of moduli spaces of polarized varieties”
Erratum to "Special subvarieties of non-arithmetic ball quotients and Hodge theory"
A phantom on a rational surface
Fano 4-folds with  $b_{2} > 12$  are products of surfaces
A prismatic approach to crystalline local systems

nodes.label: A^1-action as G_m-action with zero limit
nodes.summary: An A^1-action can be viewed as a G_m-action for which the limit as lambda approaches 0 exists. The paper stresses that this extension condition is essential.

evidence.quote: lim_{lambda -> 0} lambda x exists

nodes.label: Abelian surface
nodes.summary: A two-dimensional abelian variety. The paper studies Hitchin fibrations by comparing them with moduli spaces of sheaves on abelian surfaces.

evidence.quote: moduli space of certain torsion sheaves on an abelian surface

nodes.label: Abelian variety
nodes.summary: A complete algebraic group variety. It is the one-layer ambient notion for abelian surfaces used in the paper's main degeneration method.

evidence.quote: via abelian surfaces

nodes.label: Absolute Galois group
nodes.summary: The Galois group of a separable closure of a field over the field. It is the group represented in p-adic Galois representations.

evidence.quote: Let G_K be the absolute Galois group of K.

nodes.label: Adjacent extremal rays
nodes.summary: A relation between extremal rays that lie together in a facet or share a two-dimensional face in the cone-theoretic argument.

evidence.quote: the rays adjacent to R in \tau cannot be all contained in (E_{R_1})^\perp
We conclude that R_2 is adjacent to R

nodes.label: Adjoint divisor on the resolved contraction center
nodes.summary: The divisor class \(K_{S'}+L\) on the minimal resolution of the contraction center, used to find curves where the adjunction expression is trivial.

evidence.quote: We show that K_{S'} + L is semiample
look for curves in S' on which K_{S'} + L is trivial

nodes.label: Admissible subcategory
nodes.summary: A full triangulated subcategory of a triangulated category whose inclusion has the adjoints needed for it to occur as a component of semiorthogonal decompositions. Phantom categories in the paper are admissible subcategories of \mathsf{D}^b(X).

evidence.quote: A nontrivial admissible subcategory \mathcal{A}\subseteq \mathsf{D}^b (X) is called a phantom
if \mathcal{A} is an admissible subcategory of \mathsf{D}^b (X)

nodes.label: Affine algebraic variety
nodes.summary: An algebraic variety is affine when it is isomorphic to the spectrum of a finitely generated algebra over the base field.

evidence.quote: Given a morphism Y = Spec(B) to X = Spec(A) between affine algebraic varieties

nodes.label: Affine line as multiplicative algebraic monoid
nodes.summary: The affine line A^1 over k equipped with multiplication, used as an algebraic monoid acting on varieties and on itself.

evidence.quote: multiplicative algebraic monoid A^1

nodes.label: Algebraic base
nodes.summary: A base space carrying an algebraic-variety structure, as opposed to only an analytic or topological one. The Fixed Part Theorem is stated here as requiring such a base.

evidence.quote: Fixed Part Theorem (which requires an algebraic base)

nodes.label: Algebraic correspondence
nodes.summary: A relation between varieties represented by an algebraic cycle or intermediate variety mapping to both sides. The corrected argument only connects supersingular K3 surfaces by special degree p correspondences, which is insufficient for unirationality.

evidence.quote: one can only conclude that any two supersingular K3 surfaces in characteristic  $p \geq 5$  are connected by a sequence of very special degree  $p$  correspondences

nodes.label: Algebraic curve
nodes.summary: A one-dimensional algebraic variety over a field. The appendix works with a curve C over an algebraically closed field, together with its genus, gonality, symmetric powers, and Jacobian.

evidence.quote: Let  $C / k$  be a curve of genus  $g$
Let f\colon C \to \mathbb{P}^1 be a degree k genus g cover.

nodes.label: Algebraic cycle
nodes.summary: A formal algebraic combination of subvarieties. The paper treats conical objects as closed subsets or algebraic cycles and uses cycle intersection, pushforward, and multiplicity.

evidence.quote: closed subset, or algebraic cycle

nodes.label: Algebraic k-variety
nodes.summary: A separated scheme of finite type over a base field k. The paper uses this as the ambient class of spaces carrying A^1-actions and functions to A^1.

evidence.quote: separated k-scheme of finite type
Given a morphism Y = Spec(B) to X = Spec(A) between affine algebraic varieties

nodes.label: Ample divisor
nodes.summary: A positivity property of divisors; a Fano variety is characterized by an ample anticanonical divisor.

evidence.quote: The Fano condition is a natural positivity condition

nodes.label: Analytic locus of the prismatic site
nodes.summary: The analytic part of the prismatic site on which the paper defines its prismatic F-crystals. The excerpt names the locus but does not give its internal definition.

evidence.quote: analytic locus of the prismatic site of X
Prismatic F-crystal over the analytic locus

nodes.label: Anticanonical divisor
nodes.summary: The negative of the canonical divisor. Fano and del Pezzo conditions are expressed by positivity of anticanonical classes, and the paper compares \((-K_Y)|_S\) with \(-K_S\).

evidence.quote: compare (-K_Y)_{|S} to -K_S

nodes.label: Anticanonical pseudoheight of an exceptional collection
nodes.summary: A numerical obstruction to fullness of an exceptional collection, computed from Hom and cohomology vanishings involving the collection and the anticanonical direction. In the proof, nonnegative anticanonical pseudoheight implies the constructed collection is not full.

evidence.quote: Hence, \mathrm{ph}_{\mathrm{ac}} \geq 0 and we conclude that (5.1) is not full.

nodes.label: Anticanonical restriction on the resolved contraction center
nodes.summary: The divisor class \(L\) on the minimal resolution \(S'\) obtained by pulling back the restriction of \(-K_Y\) to the center surface \(S\). It is nef and big in the paper's setup.

evidence.quote: set L \coloneqq \mu^*((-K_Y)_{|S})
a nef and big divisor class on S'

nodes.label: Arithmetic lattice
nodes.summary: A discrete arithmetic subgroup of the rational points of an algebraic Q-group, used as the group \Gamma acting on G/M in an arithmetic quotient.

evidence.quote: $\Gamma \subset \mathbf{G}(\mathbb{Q})^{+}$  a neat arithmetic group
$\Gamma \subset \mathbf{G}(\mathbb{Q})^{+}$  a torsion-free arithmetic lattice

nodes.label: Arithmetic quotient
nodes.summary: A real analytic double quotient S_{\Gamma,G,M}=\Gamma\backslash G/M formed from the real identity component G of a connected semisimple algebraic Q-group, an arithmetic subgroup \Gamma, and a connected compact subgroup M.

evidence.quote: Arithmetic quotients are real analytic manifolds of the form  $S_{\Gamma, G, M} \coloneqq \Gamma \backslash G / M$

nodes.label: R_alg structure on an arithmetic quotient with chosen maximal compact
nodes.summary: The R_alg-definable manifold structure on S_{\Gamma,G,M} determined by a fixed maximal compact K containing M. It is characterized by requiring the quotient map restricted to every K-associated Siegel set to be R_alg-definable.

evidence.quote: We refer to  $S_{\Gamma, G, M, K}$  as the  $\mathbb{R}_{\mathrm{alg}}$ -definable manifold structure
defined so that the induced map  $\pi|_{\mathfrak{S}}$

nodes.label: Artin invariant
nodes.summary: An integer invariant classifying supersingular K3 surfaces or their Neron-Severi lattices in positive characteristic. The correction discusses moduli strata with Artin invariant sigma_0 and sigma_0 + 1.

evidence.quote: of Artin-invariants  $\sigma_0$  and  $\sigma_0 + 1$
maps surjectively onto the open set of rigidified K3 crystals of Artin invariant  $\sigma_0$

nodes.label: Balanced splitting type
nodes.summary: A splitting type whose entries are as equal as possible for a fixed rank and total degree. The excerpt uses B(k-1,|\vec e|+n) and notes maximal Brill-Noether splitting types have a balanced-plus-balanced shape.

evidence.quote: The maximal splitting types among those with r + 1 global sections have a "balanced plus balanced" shape
let us write (-n, *, \ldots, *) to denote the splitting type of \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^1}(-n) \oplus B(k - 1, |\vec{e}| + n).

nodes.label: Beilinson filtration on TP
nodes.summary: A complete exhaustive multiplicative decreasing Z-indexed filtration on TP(R) obtained from the cyclotomic Whitehead tower using the Beilinson t-structure on filtered spectra. Its graded pieces are shifts of the de Rham-Witt complex.

evidence.quote: graded pieces given by gr_i F_B TP(R) ~= W Omega_R^*[2i]

nodes.label: Big divisor
nodes.summary: A divisor class whose numerical positivity gives maximal Iitaka dimension. The divisor class \(L\) on \(S'\) is big in the paper.

evidence.quote: a nef and big divisor class on S'

nodes.label: Big open subset
aliases: open subset with codimension at least two complement
nodes.summary: An open subset U of a variety X is big when X minus U has codimension at least two.

evidence.quote: identifies V with a big open set of Y_0 (i.e. Y_0 minus V has codimension at least two)

nodes.label: Blow-up of the projective plane at ten general points
aliases: plane blow-up at ten general points
nodes.summary: The smooth rational surface X obtained by blowing up \mathbb{P}^2_{\mathbb{C}} at ten closed points lying in a suitable Zariski open general-position locus. Its divisor classes H and E_i are used to define the exceptional line bundles.

evidence.quote: Let X be the blow-up of \mathbb{P}_{\mathbb{C}}^{2} in 10 general closed points p_1, \ldots, p_{10}

nodes.label: Bounded derived category of coherent sheaves
nodes.summary: The bounded derived category formed from complexes of coherent sheaves on a variety X. The paper studies exceptional collections and admissible subcategories inside this category.

evidence.quote: denote by \mathsf{D}^b (X) the bounded derived category of coherent sheaves on X

nodes.label: Brill-Noether locus
nodes.summary: For a curve C, the Brill-Noether locus W_d^r(C) is the subset of \operatorname{Pic}^d(C) consisting of line bundles L with h^0(C,L) at least r+1.

evidence.quote: W _ {d} ^ {r} (C) = \{L \in \operatorname {P i c} ^ {d} (C): h ^ {0} (C, L) \geq r + 1 \}.

nodes.label: Brill-Noether number
nodes.summary: The expected dimension g-(r+1)(g-d+r) of the Brill-Noether locus W_d^r(C) on a general genus-g curve.

evidence.quote: \dim W _ {d} ^ {r} (C) = \rho (g, r, d) := g - (r + 1) (g - d + r)

nodes.label: Brill-Noether splitting degeneracy locus
nodes.summary: The union of Brill-Noether splitting loci with splitting types that specialize to \vec e; equivalently, a closed condition on the dimensions h^0(C,L(mH)) for all twists by the distinguished g^1_k.

evidence.quote: \overline {{\Sigma}} _ {\vec {e}} (C, f) := \bigcup_ {\vec {e} ^ {\prime} \leq \vec {e}} \Sigma_ {\vec {e}} (C, f).
splitting degeneracy loci provide a refinement of the stratification of \operatorname{Pic}^d(C) by Brill-Noether loci W_d^r(C).

nodes.label: Brill-Noether splitting locus
nodes.summary: For a degree-k cover f:C\to\mathbb{P}^1, the Brill-Noether splitting locus is the subset of \operatorname{Pic}^d(C) consisting of line bundles L whose pushforward f_*L has splitting type \vec e.

evidence.quote: We then define Brill-Noether splitting loci by \Sigma_ {\vec {e}} (C, f) := \{L \in \operatorname {P i c} ^ {d} (C): f _ {*} L \cong \mathcal {O} (\vec {e}) \}.

nodes.label: Canonical divisor
nodes.summary: A divisor class associated with the canonical sheaf of a variety; the paper uses its negative and adjoint combinations such as \(K_{S'}+L\).

evidence.quote: K_{S'} + L is semiample

nodes.label: Canonical line bundle of a curve
nodes.summary: The line bundle of holomorphic differentials on a smooth curve $C$. It appears as the twisting line bundle in a Higgs field and in the Hitchin base.

evidence.quote: \theta : \mathcal {E} \rightarrow \mathcal {E} \otimes \Omega_ {C}

nodes.label: Cartan involution from a maximal compact
nodes.summary: The Cartan involution of the Lie algebra g corresponding to a choice of maximal compact subgroup K. Compatibility of this involution with embedded Lie subalgebras is part of the corrected functoriality condition.

evidence.quote: a choice of maximal compact  $K$  is equivalent to a Cartan involution  $\theta_K$  of the Lie algebra  $\mathfrak{g}$

nodes.label: Tensor product of p-typical Cartier modules
nodes.summary: The symmetric monoidal tensor product on p-typical Cartier modules corepresenting (V,F)-bilinear maps. It has an explicit quotient presentation from (M tensor N)[V] by relations involving F and V.

evidence.quote: corepresentable by ... M boxtimes N
evidence.section: 4.2 The tensor product of classical Cartier modules

nodes.label: Center surface of a type (3,2) contraction
nodes.summary: The two-dimensional image \(S=f(E)\subset Y\) of the exceptional divisor of a type (3,2) contraction. It may be singular or nonnormal before additional hypotheses are applied.

evidence.quote: S \coloneqq f(E) \subset Y
while S can be not normal

nodes.label: Characteristic cycle of a constructible sheaf
nodes.summary: A conical algebraic cycle in the cotangent bundle associated to a constructible or perverse sheaf; in this paper the characteristic p construction is Saito's. The main bound applies polar multiplicities to CC(K).

evidence.quote: characteristic cycles for constructible sheaves on varieties of characteristic  $p$  defined by Saito

nodes.label: Transversality to the characteristic cycle
nodes.summary: A condition on a map or rational function, expressed in terms of the characteristic cycle, that replaces analytic or Whitney-stratification transversality in characteristic p.

evidence.quote: transversality conditions the map must satisfy for our argument to work, in terms of the characteristic cycle

nodes.label: Characteristic polynomial of a Higgs field
nodes.summary: The characteristic polynomial of the Higgs field $\theta$; its coefficients define the point of the Hitchin base assigned by the Hitchin fibration.

evidence.quote: sending  $(\mathcal{E},\theta)$  to the characteristic polynomial  $\mathrm{char}(\theta)$

nodes.label: Chern character
nodes.summary: The characteristic cohomology class ch_k(U) of the universal bundle; these classes generate tautological cohomology classes after integration along the curve.

evidence.quote: induces Chern characters chk(U) in H^{2k}(C x MxDol, Q)

nodes.label: Chow group
nodes.summary: The group of algebraic cycles of fixed codimension modulo rational equivalence. The appendix uses Chow groups and cycle classes to express intersection-theoretic bounds in Jacobians.

evidence.quote: Thus we have corresponding maps on Chow groups

nodes.label: Chow motive of Lefschetz type
nodes.summary: A property of a Chow motive indicating that it decomposes into Lefschetz motives. The paper uses this property of the blown-up surface, together with K0-vanishing, to prove universality of the phantom.

evidence.quote: The Chow motive of X with integer coefficients is of Lefschetz type

nodes.label: Pullback and pushforward on Chow groups
nodes.summary: The operations on Chow groups induced by the finite flat map from Sym^k C to Sym^k P^1 and the proper Abel-Jacobi map to J^(k). These operations compute theta cycle classes in the appendix.

evidence.quote: $CH^j(\mathbb{P}^k) \xrightarrow{\psi_k^*} CH^j(\operatorname{Sym}^k C) \xrightarrow{\phi_{k*}} CH^j(J^{(k)})$

nodes.label: Coherent sheaf
nodes.summary: A sheaf of modules locally presented by finitely generated modules over the structure sheaf. Coherent sheaves are the objects whose bounded derived category is denoted \mathsf{D}^b(X).

evidence.quote: bounded derived category of coherent sheaves on X

nodes.label: Compact-support motive formula for homogeneous contact loci
nodes.summary: For a homogeneous weighted equivariant f, the compactly supported motive of the contact locus X_{nu,o}(f) is expressed using f_{sigma,!} Psi_f(1_{X_eta}) with the indicated Tate twist and shift.

evidence.quote: f_sigma,! Psi_f(1_{X_eta})

nodes.label: Compatible morphism of arithmetic quotients with chosen maximal compact
aliases: morphism of arithmetic quotients with a choice of maximal compact
nodes.summary: A real semialgebraic map between arithmetic quotients induced by a Q-group morphism and right translation by g, satisfying compatibility of arithmetic subgroups, compact subgroups, maximal compact subgroups, and preservation of the source Lie algebra by the target Cartan involution.

evidence.quote: We define a morphism of arithmetic quotients with a choice of maximal compact to be a real semialgebraic map
the Cartan involution  $\theta_{gKg^{-1}}$  preserves  $\phi(\mathfrak{g}')$

nodes.label: Complete discretely valued p-adic field
nodes.summary: A complete discretely valued field of characteristic 0 with p-adic topology. The paper fixes such a field K as the base of its relative p-adic Hodge theory setting.

evidence.quote: complete discretely valued p-adic field of characteristic 0

nodes.label: Completed Cartier tensor product
nodes.summary: The tensor product on derived V-complete p-typical Cartier modules obtained by derived V-completing the ordinary Cartier tensor product. It matches the symmetric monoidal structure induced on the cyclotomic heart.

evidence.quote: M boxtimes-hat N := L(M boxtimes N)
evidence.section: 4.2 The tensor product of classical Cartier modules

nodes.label: Conical cycle on a vector bundle
nodes.summary: A closed subset or algebraic cycle in a vector bundle that is invariant under scalar dilation by the multiplicative group. Polar multiplicities are defined for such cycles in the cotangent bundle.

evidence.quote: is invariant under the  $\mathbb{G}_m$  action by dilation of vectors

nodes.label: Connected compact subgroup
nodes.summary: A connected compact subgroup M of the real Lie group G; quotienting by M produces the homogeneous space in an arithmetic quotient.

evidence.quote: $M \subset G$  a connected compact subgroup

nodes.label: Constant Newton polygon condition
nodes.summary: The condition that the Newton polygons of a convergent F-isocrystal are constant on the smooth base scheme. In the excerpt this condition guarantees existence of a slope filtration.

evidence.quote: Newton polygons ... are constant

nodes.label: Constructible derived category
nodes.summary: The bounded derived category of constructible sheaves on a variety, here with rational coefficients. It carries the perverse $t$-structure used to define perverse filtrations.

evidence.quote: constructible derived category  $D_c^b(Y)$  with rational coefficients

nodes.label: Constructible sheaf
nodes.summary: A sheaf or complex that is locally constant with finite-dimensional stalks along a finite stratification. Saito's characteristic cycle construction is invoked for constructible sheaves on varieties in characteristic p.

evidence.quote: characteristic cycles for constructible sheaves on varieties of characteristic  $p$
constructible derived category  $D_c^b(Y)$

nodes.label: Contraction of varieties
nodes.summary: A surjective morphism \(f:X\to Y\) with connected fibers, where the target is normal and projective.

evidence.quote: A contraction is a surjective morphism f:X\to Y, with connected fibers
where Y is normal and projective

nodes.label: Convergent F-isocrystal
nodes.summary: A convergent isocrystal on X/K equipped with a Frobenius structure. In the paper, slope filtrations are taken in the category of convergent F-isocrystals.

evidence.quote: convergent F-isocrystal on X / K

nodes.label: Corrected S2 incidence set
nodes.summary: The corrected set \(S_2\) consists of triples \(((\{(i,j),(j,l)\}),(i,l))\in I^{(2)}\times J\) satisfying a special midpoint rule: if \(\{i,l\}=\{d-1,d\}\), then \(j=\lfloor(i+l)/2\rfloor\); otherwise \(j=\lceil(i+l)/2\rceil+\delta_{i,l}\).

evidence.quote: The right description of  \(S_{2}\)  is
\(S _ {2} = \left\{\left(\left\{(i, j), (j, l) \right\}, (i, l)\right) \in I ^ {(2)} \times J \mid \{i, l \} = \{d - 1, d \} \text {a n d} j = \left\lfloor \frac {i + l}{2} \right\rfloor ,\)

nodes.label: Cotangent bundle
nodes.summary: The vector bundle of cotangent directions on a smooth variety X. Characteristic cycles and the conical cycles whose polar multiplicities are used in the main bound live in this bundle.

evidence.quote: cotangent bundle  $T^{*}X$  of  $X$

nodes.label: Counterexample to a mathematical claim
nodes.summary: An example showing that a stated mathematical assertion is false. The retraction cites Kollár's counterexample to the main result of the retracted paper.

evidence.quote: Kollár has published a counterexample to the main result

nodes.label: C_p norm map
nodes.summary: The norm map from C_p-homotopy orbits to C_p-homotopy fixed points of an S^1-spectrum. A topological Cartier module is defined by factoring this norm through a spectrum M.

evidence.quote: factorization of the C_p-norm
evidence.section: 3.1 The infinity-category of topological Cartier modules

nodes.label: Crystalline cohomology
nodes.summary: A cohomology theory for schemes in characteristic p that the paper recovers through de Rham-Witt complexes and filtrations on TP. The excerpt uses it through its computation by the de Rham-Witt complex rather than through the crystalline site definition.

evidence.quote: de Rham-Witt complex, which computes crystalline cohomology
crystalline cohomology of the special fiber X_s

nodes.label: Crystalline Galois representation
nodes.summary: A p-adic Galois representation that becomes controlled by a filtered Frobenius module after base extension to B_crys. The paper frames crystalline local systems as a geometric relative analogue.

evidence.quote: crystalline Galois representations, which are roughly \mathbf{Q}_p-representations
after a base extension to \mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{crys}}

nodes.label: Curious Hard Lefschetz property
nodes.summary: The Lefschetz-type symmetry for character varieties used here to force the perverse and even weight filtrations to coincide once one filtration is contained in the other.

evidence.quote: The curious hard Lefschetz theorem proven by Mellit forces the two filtrations P and W2 to coincide

nodes.label: Curve-class subspace of a closed subset
nodes.summary: For a closed subset \(Z\subset X\), the subspace of \(\mathcal N_1(X)\) spanned by numerical classes of curves contained in \(Z\).

evidence.quote: \mathcal{N}_1(Z,X):=\iota_*(\mathcal{N}_1(Z)) \subset \mathcal{N}_1(X)
spanned by classes of curves in Z

nodes.label: Multiplicity of a cycle at a point
nodes.summary: The local multiplicity of an algebraic cycle at a point, computed as the corresponding linear combination of multiplicities of its irreducible components.

evidence.quote: the multiplicity of an algebraic cycle at a point

nodes.label: Cycle-theoretic pullback of a prime divisor
nodes.summary: The cycle-theoretic pullback of a prime divisor records the codimension-one pullback cycle with multiplicities.

evidence.quote: let P be a prime divisor on X, and let Q=f^*P be the cycle-theoretic pullback

nodes.label: Heart of the cyclotomic t-structure
nodes.summary: The abelian heart of the cyclotomic t-structure. The paper identifies it with the full subcategory of derived V-complete p-typical Cartier modules.

evidence.quote: heart ... equivalent to ... derived V-complete Cartier modules

nodes.label: Cyclotomic homotopy object
nodes.summary: For a p-typical cyclotomic spectrum X, pi_i^cyc X denotes its ith homotopy object with respect to the cyclotomic t-structure. These objects lie in the cyclotomic heart and may be read as derived V-complete Cartier modules.

evidence.quote: denoted by pi_i^cyc X

nodes.label: Cyclotomic t-structure
nodes.summary: The t-structure on p-typical cyclotomic spectra whose connective part consists of objects whose underlying spectrum is connective. Its heart is identified with derived V-complete p-typical Cartier modules.

evidence.quote: connective part of a t-structure on CycSp_p

nodes.label: Cyclotomic Whitehead tower
nodes.summary: The tower of cyclotomic truncations associated to the cyclotomic t-structure. Applying TP to this tower gives a filtration on TP(R).

evidence.quote: filtration on TP(R) induced by the cyclotomic Whitehead tower

nodes.label: de Rham-Witt complex
nodes.summary: The complex W Omega_R^* whose ith term is W Omega_R^i. For smooth algebras over perfect fields of characteristic p, it identifies with the cyclotomic homotopy objects of THH(R) as a Dieudonne complex.

evidence.quote: W Omega_R^i is the ith term in the de Rham-Witt complex

nodes.label: Degree-k cover of the projective line
nodes.summary: A finite morphism from a smooth genus-g curve C to \mathbb{P}^1 of degree k. It determines a distinguished linear series and pushforwards of line bundles to vector bundles on \mathbb{P}^1.

evidence.quote: Let f\colon C \to \mathbb{P}^1 be a degree k genus g cover.
smooth degree k, genus g covers f\colon C\to \mathbb{P}^1

nodes.label: Degree p multisection
nodes.summary: A multisection of a family whose projection to the base is finite flat of degree p. The correction proves existence of such a multisection but not that it can be chosen radicial over the base.

evidence.quote: There exists a degree  $p$  multisection  $D_{\xi} \subset \overline{A}_{\xi}$  such that the induced morphism $D _ {\xi} \rightarrow \xi \times_ {k} S$ is finite and flat of degree  $p$
one may not be able to choose the degree  $p$  multisection  $D_{\xi}$  to be radicial

nodes.label: Del Pezzo surface
nodes.summary: A smooth projective Fano surface, equivalently a smooth surface with ample anticanonical divisor. The paper proves that high-Picard-number smooth Fano 4-folds are products of such surfaces.

evidence.quote: X \cong S_1 \times S_2, where S_i are del Pezzo surfaces
S is a smooth del Pezzo surface with -K_S = (-K_Y)_{|S}

nodes.label: Delta-regularity for spectral curves
nodes.summary: A Severi-type inequality for spectral curves relating codimension in the Hitchin base to the delta invariant of the relative Picard or Prym group scheme.

evidence.quote: Then we have the Severi inequality, referred to as delta-regularity, for the spectral curves.

nodes.label: Derived (p,I)-complete ring
nodes.summary: A derived completeness condition with respect to the ideal generated by p and I. It is one of the defining conditions in the excerpt's description of prisms.

evidence.quote: (derived) (p, I)-complete

nodes.label: Derived V-complete Cartier module
nodes.summary: A p-typical Cartier module M for which the derived inverse limit of the V-tower vanishes, equivalently the canonical map M -> lim_n cofib(V^n:M->M) is an equivalence in D(Z). This is the intrinsic algebraic condition defining the cyclotomic heart.

evidence.quote: M -> lim_n M / V^n is an equivalence

nodes.label: dg-enhancement
nodes.summary: A dg-category whose homotopy category recovers a given triangulated category. The paper notes that admissible subcategories in the relevant setting have dg-enhancements quasi-equivalent to categories of perfect dg-modules.

evidence.quote: \mathcal{A} has a dg-enhancement quasi-equivalent to Perf-\mathcal{R}

nodes.label: Dieudonne complex
aliases: Dieudonne complex in the paper's sense
nodes.summary: A p-typical Cartier complex satisfying the additional relations V F = p and eta = 0. Cyclotomic homotopy of THH-modules over THH(F_p) naturally takes values in such complexes.

evidence.quote: p-typical Cartier complex ... with V F = p and eta = 0
evidence.section: 6.2 THH of smooth schemes over perfect rings in characteristic p

nodes.label: Direct summand of an affine variety
nodes.summary: For a morphism Spec(B) to Spec(A), X is a direct summand of Y when A is a direct summand of B as an A-module.

evidence.quote: we say that X is a direct summand of Y if A is a direct summand of B as A-modules

nodes.label: Distinguished gonal linear series
nodes.summary: For a degree-k cover f:C\to\mathbb{P}^1, the pullback H=f^*\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^1}(1) is the distinguished degree-k linear series on C used to test splitting degeneracy conditions.

evidence.quote: Let H = f^{*}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}^{1}}(1) be the distinguished g_{k}^{1} on C.

nodes.label: Divisor class on a surface
nodes.summary: A divisor modulo the chosen equivalence relation, used to specify line bundles on a surface. In the paper the classes H, E_i, D_i, and F determine the terms of the line bundle collection.

evidence.quote: Denote by H the divisor class obtained by pulling back the class of a hyperplane
denote by E_i the class of the exceptional divisor over the point p_i

nodes.label: Divisor on a variety
nodes.summary: A codimension-one cycle or divisor class on a variety, used for canonical classes, exceptional divisors, intersection products, and positivity conditions.

evidence.quote: since E_{R_1} is an effective divisor
a nef and big divisor class on S'

nodes.label: Divisorial contraction
nodes.summary: An elementary contraction whose exceptional locus is a divisor. The paper focuses on divisorial elementary contractions with two-dimensional image of the exceptional divisor.

evidence.quote: an elementary contraction can be of fiber type, divisorial, or small
it is divisorial with \dim S = 2

nodes.label: Divisorial part of a scheme-theoretic pullback
nodes.summary: For the scheme-theoretic pullback of a divisor, its divisorial part is the codimension-one cycle retained from that pullback.

evidence.quote: let Q be the divisorial part of the scheme-theoretic pullback f^{-1}(P)

nodes.label: Divisorial section algebra
aliases: section ring of a Weil divisor
nodes.summary: For a Weil divisor D on X, the divisorial section algebra is the graded algebra R(X,D) formed from global sections of O_X(mD) for nonnegative integers m.

nodes.label: Poincare duality for prismatic cohomology

Poincaré duality, and Frobenius isogeny

nodes.label: Polar-multiplicity bound for perverse-sheaf stalks
nodes.summary: The characteristic p inequality bounding each stalk cohomology dimension of a perverse sheaf by the corresponding polar multiplicity of its characteristic cycle.

nodes.label: Polar multiplicity of a conical cycle
nodes.summary: For an n-dimensional conical cycle C in T^*X and a point x, the i-th polar multiplicity is the multiplicity at x of the pushforward of the intersection of projectivized C with a general rank i+1 subbundle. The top polar multiplicity is the multiplicity of the zero section in C.

evidence.quote: we define the  $i$ th polar multiplicity of  $C$  at  $x$

nodes.label: Polarized variety
nodes.summary: A variety together with a polarization, typically an ample line bundle or ample divisor class used to rigidify moduli problems.

evidence.quote: moduli spaces of polarized varieties

nodes.label: Polygraph attached to a graph

evidence.quote: Then,  \(\Gamma_{2}\)  is the polygraph attached to the graph  \(\Gamma_3 = (I,E)\)

nodes.label: Positive curvature current
nodes.summary: The curvature current associated to a singular hermitian metric, required to be positive as a current. The retraction identifies its Lelong numbers as the critical obstruction.

evidence.quote: the positive curvature current of the singular hermitian metric

nodes.label: Positive root chamber cone

nodes.label: Preservation of strong perversity under vanishing cycles

nodes.label: Prism

endomorphism on A lifting Frobenius on A / p

nodes.label: Prismatic cohomology

nodes.label: Prismatic-crystalline comparison

nodes.label: Prismatic-etale comparison
nodes.summary: A comparison between prismatic cohomology and p-adic etale cohomology. The excerpt presents etale cohomology as a specialization of prismatic structure-sheaf cohomology.

evidence.quote: including \mathbf{Z}_p-étale cohomology of the generic fiber X_{\eta}

nodes.label: Prismatic F-crystal
nodes.summary: A crystal on the analytic locus of the prismatic site equipped with Frobenius data. It is the prismatic object equivalent to integral crystalline local systems in the paper's main result.

evidence.quote: prismatic F-crystals over the analytic locus

nodes.label: Prismatic site
nodes.summary: A Grothendieck site attached to a smooth p-adic formal scheme X whose objects are maps from prisms to X. Its structure sheaf cohomology specializes to p-adic etale and crystalline cohomology.

nodes.label: Product of del Pezzo surfaces

X \cong S_1 \times S_2, where S_i are del Pezzo surfaces

nodes.label: Projective line

a map  $\pi : C \to \mathbb{P}^1$  of degree  $r$
smooth degree k, genus g covers f\colon C\to \mathbb{P}^1

n general points in \mathbb{P}_{\mathbb{C}}^{2}

nodes.label: Proper morphism

evidence.quote: \rho : \pi_ {1} (C \setminus \{p \}) \rightarrow \operatorname {G L} _ {r}

nodes.label: Pure Hodge manifold

nodes.label: Preservation of formal klt type under pure images

nodes.label: Preservation of formal plt type under pure images
aliases: pure images of formally plt type pairs

nodes.label: Preservation of klt type under pure images
aliases: direct summands of klt type singularities are klt type
pure images of klt type singularities

nodes.label: Preservation of plt type under pure images
aliases: pure images of plt type pairs

evidence.quote: Assume that (Y, Q) is of plt type. Then (X, P) is of plt type.

nodes.label: Pure polarizable Q-Hodge structure

nodes.label: Pure subring

nodes.label: Pushforward of a line bundle under a cover

nodes.label: Tautological cohomology class
nodes.summary: A natural cohomology class on the relevant moduli spaces, treated as a generator whose expected perverse degree is predicted by P=W. The excerpt mentions these classes but does not define their universal construction.

evidence.quote: all tautological generators lie in the correct pieces of the perverse filtration
The tautological classes ck(gamma) are defined to be ... qM*(qC* gamma cup chk(U))

nodes.label: Tautological line bundle on parabolic Hitchin moduli
nodes.summary: For a character xi of the maximal torus, the B-reduction in a parabolic Higgs bundle yields a T-torsor and hence a tautological line bundle L(xi) on the parabolic moduli stack.

evidence.quote: the B-reduction ... yields a T-torsor over Mxpar which induces for each xi ... a line bundle L(xi)

nodes.label: t-structure on TCart_p
nodes.summary: The t-structure on the infinity-category of p-typical topological Cartier modules whose connective and coconnective objects are detected by the underlying spectrum. Its heart consists of discrete topological Cartier modules, i.e. p-typical Cartier modules.

evidence.quote: underlying spectrum is connective

nodes.label: Theta class on a Picard variety
nodes.summary: The cohomology or Chow class of a theta divisor on a Picard variety. In the paper, Chern classes of pushforwards over \operatorname{Pic}^d(C) are multiples of powers of \theta and splitting classes are expressed as a_{\vec e}\theta^{u(\vec e)}.

evidence.quote: The i th Chern class of this vector bundle is a multiple of \theta^i that does not depend on g
[\overline{\Sigma}_{\vec{e}}(C', f')] = a_{\vec{e}}\theta^{u(\vec{e})} \neq 0

nodes.label: Theta locus in a Jacobian
nodes.summary: The locus of degree-k effective divisor classes in the corresponding Picard or Jacobian component, equivalently the image of the k-th symmetric power of the curve.

evidence.quote: $\Theta_k \subset J^{(k)}$  the image of  $\operatorname{Sym}^k C$

nodes.label: Intersection of translated theta loci
nodes.summary: The intersection of two translates of theta loci in a hyperelliptic Jacobian. Bounding the Betti numbers of these intersections is the Shende-Tsimerman application of the paper.

evidence.quote: intersections of two translates of theta loci in a hyperelliptic Jacobian

nodes.label: Betti numbers of theta-translate intersections
nodes.summary: The cohomological Betti numbers of intersections of two translated theta loci in a hyperelliptic Jacobian. The paper applies the perverse-sheaf stalk bound to prove the predicted bounds for these numbers.

evidence.quote: Betti numbers of the intersections of two translates of theta loci

nodes.label: Thom space of a vector bundle
nodes.summary: For a vector bundle E on S with projection p and zero section s, its Thom space in SH(S) is p_sharp s_* 1_S.

evidence.quote: Th(E) = p_sharp s_* 1_S

nodes.label: Thom twist in motivic stable homotopy
nodes.summary: The operation of tensoring an object in SH with the Thom space of a vector bundle, written Th(M) tensor A in the paper.

evidence.quote: Th(M) tensor A|_{X_eta}

nodes.label: Topological Hochschild homology
nodes.summary: Topological Hochschild homology of ring spectra and schemes, regarded in this paper as a p-typical cyclotomic spectrum. The cyclotomic t-structure makes THH(k) discrete for perfect characteristic p rings and identifies its cyclotomic homotopy with Witt and de Rham-Witt data.

evidence.quote: THH(R) in (CycSp_p)_{0}

nodes.label: Topological periodic cyclic homology
nodes.summary: The topological periodic cyclic homology object obtained by applying the S^1-Tate construction to THH. The paper studies filtrations on TP(R) induced by the cyclotomic t-structure and compares them with BMS filtrations.

evidence.quote: Applying the S^1-Tate construction ... on TP(R)

nodes.label: Torsor under a group scheme
nodes.summary: A scheme with a simply transitive action of a group scheme, locally trivial in an appropriate topology. The correction replaces the claimed radicial multisection by one arising from a family of A_xi[p]-torsors.

evidence.quote: be a family of  $A$ -torsors associated to a non-trivial  $p$ -torsion element
$D_{\xi}\rightarrow \xi \times_{k}S$  arises from a family of  $A_{\xi}[p]$ -torsors

nodes.label: TR functor
nodes.summary: A functor from p-typical cyclotomic spectra to p-typical topological Cartier modules, built as an inverse limit of the TR_n constructions with natural Frobenius and Verschiebung maps. It is right adjoint to the quotient-by-V functor.

evidence.quote: TR: CycSp_p -> TCart_p is right adjoint to (-)/V
evidence.section: 3.3 Topological Cartier modules and cyclotomic spectra

nodes.label: Transitive G-space with compact stabilizers
nodes.summary: A topological space with continuous transitive left G-action and connected compact stabilizers. Choosing a basepoint identifies it with G/M, and an equivariant map to X gives a definable structure on its arithmetic quotient.

evidence.quote: a  $G$ -space as a topological space  $Y$  with a continuous transitive left  $G$ -action and connected compact stabilizers

nodes.label: Triangulated category
nodes.summary: An additive category equipped with a shift functor and distinguished triangles satisfying the standard triangulated axioms. Semiorthogonal decompositions and exceptional collections are formulated inside triangulated categories.

evidence.quote: the smallest triangulated subcategory of \mathsf{D}^b(X)

nodes.label: Twisted character variety
nodes.summary: The affine moduli space of irreducible $\mathrm{GL}_r$ local systems on the punctured curve satisfying the specified scalar monodromy condition around the puncture.

evidence.quote: The second moduli space is the (twisted) character variety  $\mathcal{M}_B$
The Betti moduli space MB is the (twisted) character variety associated with GLn(C) and degree d.

nodes.label: Twisted monodromy condition
nodes.summary: The condition that the local monodromy around the puncture equals the scalar matrix $\xi_r^\chi I_r$. It is the twisting datum in the Betti moduli space.

evidence.quote: sends a loop around a chosen point  $p$  to  $\xi_r^\chi I_r$

nodes.label: Elementary contraction of type (3,2)
nodes.summary: A divisorial elementary contraction \(f:X\to Y\) of a Fano 4-fold whose exceptional divisor has image a surface \(S=f(E)\). Such contractions are central to the proof.

evidence.quote: is of type (3, 2) if it is divisorial with \dim S = 2
The proof relies on a careful study of elementary contractions of X of type (3, 2)

nodes.label: Unipotent radical
nodes.summary: The maximal connected unipotent normal subgroup of a parabolic algebraic group. In the paper it supplies the N(R)-factor U in a Siegel set.

evidence.quote: with unipotent radical  $\mathbf{N}$

nodes.label: Unirational variety
nodes.summary: A variety is unirational if it is dominated by a rational variety, equivalently if there is a dominant rational map from some projective space to it. The corrected paper says the previous proof no longer establishes this property for all supersingular K3 surfaces.

evidence.quote: the main result, the unirationality of supersingular K3 surfaces [2, Theorem 5.3], remains a conjecture
one needs Assertion (4) of [2, Theorem 3.6], whose proof requires that one can find degree  p  multisections

nodes.label: Unit object in motivic stable homotopy
nodes.summary: The tensor unit 1_S in SH(S), used as the constant motivic object and in the construction of Thom spaces.

nodes.label: Universal PGL_n-bundle
nodes.summary: The universal PGL_n-bundle over the product of the curve with the PGL_n Dolbeault moduli space or its L-twisted variant.

evidence.quote: The universal PGLn-bundle U on C x MxDol induces Chern characters

nodes.label: Universal phantom category
nodes.summary: A phantom category whose phantom behavior persists universally, typically after tensoring or taking products with derived categories of smooth projective varieties. The paper proves the orthogonal complement is a universal phantom.

evidence.quote: the orthogonal complement of this collection is a universal phantom category
\mathcal{A} ... is a universal phantom subcategory of \mathsf{D}^b(X)

nodes.label: Universal phantom criterion via kappa-motive
nodes.summary: A criterion saying that, under suitable motivic hypotheses such as Lefschetz-type Chow motive and K0-vanishing, vanishing of the kappa-motive implies that an admissible phantom is universal.

evidence.quote: By [6, Cor. 4.3] the \kappa-motive of \mathcal{A} with integer coefficients vanishes and [6, Prop. 4.4] shows that \mathcal{A} is a universal phantom.

nodes.label: Universal splitting degeneracy class
nodes.summary: A universal Chern-class formula for the expected class of a splitting degeneracy locus, depending only on the splitting type and the Chern classes of pushforward bundles \pi_*\mathcal{E}(m) for sufficiently large m.

evidence.quote: the expected class of \overline{\Sigma}_{\vec{e}}(C,f) is given by a universal formula, depending only on \vec{e}, in terms of the Chern classes of \pi_{*}\mathcal{E}(m) for suitably large m.

nodes.label: V-complete topological Cartier module
nodes.summary: A bounded below p-typical topological Cartier module M for which the inverse limit of the tower built from iterated V maps on C_p-homotopy orbits vanishes. For bounded below objects this agrees with mod V-locality and is characterized by derived V-completeness of homotopy groups.

evidence.quote: is V-complete if the limit of the tower ... vanishes
evidence.section: 3.3 Topological Cartier modules and cyclotomic spectra

nodes.label: Vanishing cycle functor
nodes.summary: For a morphism g:X -> A^1, the vanishing cycle functor maps constructible complexes on X to complexes on the special fiber and preserves perverse t-structures.

evidence.quote: the vanishing cycle functor ... preserves the perverse t-structures

nodes.label: Vanishing Lelong numbers
nodes.summary: The property that all Lelong numbers of a positive current vanish. The retraction states that the relevant curvature current does not generally satisfy this property.

evidence.quote: does not have vanishing Lelong numbers

nodes.label: Vector bundle
nodes.summary: A locally free family of vector spaces over a base variety. The paper defines conical cycles and projectivization for vector bundles.

evidence.quote: For a vector bundle  $V$  on a variety  $X$

nodes.label: Vector bundle on a curve
nodes.summary: A locally free sheaf of finite rank on a curve. In the excerpt, the bundle $\mathcal E$ is the underlying object of a Higgs bundle and has fixed rank and Euler characteristic.

evidence.quote: with  $\mathrm{rank}(\mathcal{E}) = r$  and  $\chi (\mathcal{E}) = \chi$

nodes.label: Vector bundle on a scheme
nodes.summary: A vector bundle E over a scheme S, with projection p: E -> S and zero section s. The paper uses it to build Thom spaces.

evidence.quote: vector bundle on a scheme S

nodes.label: Splitting type of a vector bundle on the projective line
nodes.summary: The nondecreasing integer tuple \vec e=(e_1,...,e_k) such that a rank-k vector bundle on \mathbb{P}^1 is isomorphic to \mathcal{O}(e_1)\oplus\cdots\oplus\mathcal{O}(e_k).

evidence.quote: Every vector bundle on \mathbb{P}^1 is isomorphic to \mathcal{O}(e_1) \oplus \dots \oplus \mathcal{O}(e_k) for some collection of integers \vec{e} = (e_1, \ldots, e_k) with e_1 \leq \dots \leq e_k. We call such a collection \vec{e} the splitting type

nodes.label: (V,F)-bilinear pairing
nodes.summary: A bilinear pairing of p-typical Cartier modules compatible with Frobenius and Verschiebung: F(x,y)=(F x,F y), V(x,F y)=(V x,y), and V(F x,y)=(x,V y). It is the universal property used to define the Cartier tensor product.

nodes.label: Virtual motive

evidence.quote: virtual motives

nodes.label: Weak abelian fibration
